name: product_page_3_products_rest-starter-kit

observed_strengths.observation: 'How it Stacks Up' H2 comparison section — in-page competitor or alternative comparison on PDP directly addresses 'why not just take melatonin/Calm/ZzzQuil' objection, a key conversion-blocker for a zero-caffeine sleep product

Zero-caffeine positioning with named ingredients (Rooibos, Valerian, Passionflower) — strong functional differentiation from the energy blends, indicates breadth of use-cases (morning energy + nighttime calm) within the same brand

Rest Starter Kit as a named bundle — consistent starter kit merchandising strategy across the catalog (Original, Coffee, Matcha, Turmeric, Rest) lowers AOV by positioning multi-product trial as the default entry point

missing_or_weak.observation: No 'Reviews' H2 visible — same gap as all 3 sampled PDPs; reviews are not confirmed as visible in the above-fold heading structure of any crawled PDP
No cross-sell from Rest to Energy blend on PDP — 'Morning + Evening ritual bundle' pairing (Rest + Original/Coffee) is a natural high-AOV combination that isn't surfaced on the Rest PDP

'Buy now' cross-links to coffee-starter-kit — same sitewide pattern; on Rest PDP this creates specific confusion (you're reading about zero-caffeine Rest and Buy Now takes you to a caffeinated coffee product)

purchase_path_notes: Rest Starter Kit demonstrates strong use-case differentiation. The 'How it Stacks Up' comparison section is a CRO asset unique to this PDP. Cross-sell from Rest to Energy (morning + evening bundle) is the primary missed AOV opportunity.
